questions.text: What gameplay scope should v0.1 ship with?
options: Single-player marathon only (endless with increasing speed)
Marathon + Sprint (40 lines) + Ultra (2-minute score attack)
Single-player + local hotseat versus on one terminal
Single-player + networked head-to-head versus over TCP

questions.text: Which Tetris rule set and rotation system should we implement?
options: Modern guideline: SRS rotation, 7-bag randomizer, hold piece, ghost piece, lock delay
Classic NES-style: no hold, no ghost, simple rotation, gravity-only lock
Hybrid: SRS + 7-bag + ghost, but no hold and no T-spin detection

questions.text: What Rust TUI rendering approach should we use?
options: Raw crossterm with a hand-rolled double-buffered renderer

questions.text: How should input handling and the game loop be structured?
options: Fixed-timestep tick thread (~60Hz) + separate input thread via channels
Async tokio loop with select! over input and tick timers
Single-threaded poll loop with crossterm event timeout for frame pacing
ECS-style (bevy_ecs headless) with a fixed schedule

questions.text: What persistence and configuration should v0.1 include?
options: Local high-score file (JSON) in XDG config dir, no configuration
High scores + TOML config for keybindings and DAS/ARR tuning
No persistence — session-only scores, hardcoded controls
High scores + replay files (seed + input log) for playback
